name: Christopher Lee
description:
'''Sir Christopher Frank Carandini Lee''' (27 May 1922 – 7 June 2015) was an English actor and singer. In a career spanning over 60 years, he became known as an actor with tremendous screen presence and a deep and commanding voice, who often portrayed villains in horror and franchise films. Lee was [[Knight Bachelor|knighted]] for services to drama and charity in June 2009 by [[Charles III|Charles]], then the [[Prince of Wales]], and received the [[BAFTA Fellowship]] in 2011 and the [[British Film Institute Fellowship|BFI Fellowship]] in 2013.

Lee gained fame for portraying [[Count Dracula]] in seven [[Hammer Film Productions|Hammer Horror]] films, beginning with ''[[Dracula (1958 film)|Dracula]]'' (1958). He frequently appeared opposite his friend [[Peter Cushing]] in horror films, including Hammer's ''[[The Curse of Frankenstein]]'' (1957) and ''[[The Mummy (1959 film)|The Mummy]]'' (1959). His other film roles include [[Francisco Scaramanga]] in the [[Production of the James Bond films|James Bond film]] ''[[The Man with the Golden Gun (film)|The Man with the Golden Gun]]'' (1974), [[Count Dooku]] in two [[List of Star Wars films|''Star Wars'' films]] (2002–2005), and [[Saruman]] in both [[The Lord of the Rings (film series)|''The Lord of the Rings'' film trilogy]] (2001–2003) and [[The Hobbit (film series)|''The Hobbit'' film trilogy]] (2012–2014). He had roles in the [[List of frequent Tim Burton collaborators|Tim Burton films]] ''[[Sleepy Hollow (film)|Sleepy Hollow]]'' (1999), ''[[Corpse Bride]]'' (2005), ''[[Charlie and the Chocolate Factory (film)|Charlie and the Chocolate Factory]]'' (2005), ''[[Alice in Wonderland (2010 film)|Alice in Wonderland]]'' (2010), and ''[[Dark Shadows (film)|Dark Shadows]]'' (2012). His other notable films include ''[[A Tale of Two Cities (1958 film)|A Tale of Two Cities]]'' (1958), ''[[Castle of the Living Dead]]'' (1964), ''[[The Wicker Man]]'' (1973), ''[[The Return of Captain Invincible]]'' (1983), ''[[Gremlins 2: The New Batch]]'' (1990), ''[[Jinnah (film)|Jinnah]]'' (1998), ''[[Glorious 39]]'' (2009), and ''[[Hugo (film)|Hugo]]'' (2011).

In addition to his prolific film career, Lee was a classically trained singer with a passion for [[Heavy metal music|heavy metal]]. He recorded several albums, including the symphonic metal concept albums ''[[Charlemagne: By the Sword and the Cross]]'' (2010) and ''[[Charlemagne: The Omens of Death]]'' (2013), portraying the title character of Charlemagne. These projects featured Lee providing spoken word, singing, and dramatic narration.
